user_query:
Please read input/manifest.json and input/idea.json. Build a complete JSON-RPC EXECUTE_PIPELINE request for an "idea-to-publish" pipeline with these exact steps and actions: spark (create_note), expand (ai_expand), visual (generate_figure), verify (wave_check), assemble (merge_notes), export (export_pdf). Wire dependencies using the $ref syntax exactly as follows:
- spark.params.title = "$INPUT.title" and spark.params.content = "$INPUT.description"
- expand.params.note_id = "$spark.output.id"
- visual.params.note_id = "$expand.output.id"
- verify.params.content = "$expand.output.content"
- assemble.params.ids contains "$spark.output.id" and "$expand.output.id"; assemble.params.title = "DRAFT: $INPUT.title"
- export.params.note_id = "$assemble.output.id"
Set coherence_threshold to at least 0.85 and include a non-empty atom_token string.

Write the request to output/execution/execute_pipeline.json.

Then create a simulated run transcript in output/execution/transcript.jsonl containing, in order, STEP_COMPLETE notifications for each step (spark, expand, visual, verify, assemble, export) and a final COHERENCE_REPORT. Use a consistent pipeline_id across all messages. Each STEP_COMPLETE must include: pipeline_id, step_id, status="complete", output (object), wave_score between 0 and 1, and duration_ms as a positive integer. For verify's output include score (0..1) and pass (boolean). For export's output include path (string) and size_bytes (positive integer). The COHERENCE_REPORT must include overall_score >= coherence_threshold, step_scores for all six steps, conservation_check with integer alpha and omega that sum to 15 and valid=true, and an atom_trail_id string.

Also write output/execution/readme.txt with a single line listing the step ids in topological order separated by commas: spark,expand,visual,verify,assemble,export.

Do not connect to any service; this is an offline dry-run.
